lede: A look at August 4 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Lil Skies and Meghan, Duchess of Sussex, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2020 Beirut explosion
events.desc: Beirut Port explosion: At least 220 people are killed and over 5,000 are wounded when 2,700 tons of ammonium nitrate explodes in Beirut, Lebanon.

events.title: Jean-Baptiste Ouédraogo
events.desc: Jean-Baptiste Ouédraogo, president of the military government of Upper Volta, is ousted from power in a coup d'état led by Captain Thomas Sankara.

events.title: Vietnam War
events.desc: Vietnam War: At the apartment of French intermediary Jean Sainteny in Paris, American representative Henry Kissinger and North Vietnamese representative Xuân Thuỷ begin secret peace negotiations. The negotiations will eventually fail.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: In response to the German invasion of Belgium, Belgium and the British Empire declare war on Germany. The United States declares its neutrality.

events.title: Lizzie Borden
events.desc: The father and stepmother of Lizzie Borden are found murdered in their Fall River, Massachusetts home. She will be tried and acquitted for the crimes a year later.

events.title: French Revolutionary Wars
events.desc: French Revolutionary Wars: Napoleon leads the French Army of Italy to victory in the Battle of Lonato.

events.title: Treaty of Sistova
events.desc: The Treaty of Sistova is signed, ending the Ottoman–Habsburg wars.

events.title: Mount Asama
events.desc: Mount Asama erupts in Japan, killing about 1,400 people (Tenmei eruption). The eruption causes a famine, which results in an additional 20,000 deaths.

events.title: War of the Spanish Succession
events.desc: War of the Spanish Succession: Gibraltar is captured by an English and Dutch fleet, commanded by Admiral Sir George Rooke and allied with Archduke Charles.

events.title: Second Barons' War
events.desc: Second Barons' War: Battle of Evesham: The army of Prince Edward (the future king Edward I of England) defeats the forces of rebellious barons led by Simon de Montfort, 6th Earl of Leicester, killing de Montfort and many of his allies.

births.name: Meghan, Duchess of Sussex
births.line: American member of British royal family, media personality and actress

births.name: Silvan Shalom
births.line: Tunisian-Israeli sergeant and politician, 30th Deputy Prime Minister of Israel

births.name: Vicente Álvarez Areces
births.line: Spanish politician, 6th President of the Principality of Asturias (died 2019)

births.name: Knut Hamsun
births.line: Norwegian novelist, poet, and playwright, Nobel Prize laureate (died 1952)

deaths.text: Renato Ruggiero, Italian lawyer and politician, Italian Minister of Foreign Affairs
Frederick Chapman Robbins, American pediatrician and virologist, Nobel Prize laureate
Margarito Bautista, Nahua-Mexican evangelizer, theologian, and religious founder
Samuel J. Tilden, American lawyer and politician, 25th Governor of New York
William Cecil, 1st Baron Burghley, English academic and politician, Lord High Treasurer

observances.text: Christian feast day: Sithney, patron saint of mad dogs
